title: Incoming requests dashboard view for recipients
description: Recipients (users who have been sent share requests) need a view in their dashboard showing all pending and completed requests sent to their email address. This view should show pending requests with a 'Fulfill' button that links to /onboard/{token}, and completed requests showing what was shared. This is the mirror of the requester's responses view.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard has a tab or section 'Requests received' (or similar) visible to all users
Pending requests show: requester company name (from requester_company_id join), request date, field count, document count, and a 'Fulfill' button linking to /onboard/{token}
Completed requests show: requester company name, completion date, a 'Completed' badge
Expired requests show with an 'Expired' badge
Empty state shown when no requests received
tsc reports no new type errors
Playwright screenshot shows the incoming requests view

steps.details: Create a new component src/components/dashboard/IncomingRequestsList.tsx. Add a useQuery that fetches: supabase.from('share_requests').select('*, requester:requester_company_id(legal_name, contact_name, contact_email)').eq('recipient_email', user.email).order('created_at', { ascending: false }). Type the result properly using ShareRequestRow.

Render a Table with columns: From (requester legal_name), Requested ('{N} fields, {M} documents'), Date, Status (badge), Action. For pending rows: show a Button 'Fulfill' that uses router.push('/onboard/' + request.token). For completed: show 'Completed' badge. For expired: 'Expired' badge with muted styling.
If no requests returned, show a Card with an icon and message: 'No incoming requests yet. When someone requests your business information, it will appear here.'

steps.description: Add the component to the dashboard layout
steps.details: Find where the dashboard renders the request/response tabs (likely AdminDashboard.tsx or dashboard/page.tsx). Add a new tab or section for 'Received' that renders IncomingRequestsList. Ensure it's visible to both admin and external role users.
Run npx tsc --noEmit and npx eslint --fix. Start dev server. Navigate to dashboard. Take screenshot .agent/screenshots/TASK-12-incoming.png. Verify incoming requests section renders.

technicalNotes: RLS policy 'share_requests_select_by_token' allows recipients to read by matching recipient_email — but querying by email directly also needs a policy. Check supabase-schema.sql: the policy 'share_requests_select_by_token' uses FOR SELECT USING (recipient_email = (SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid())) — this allows the query without a token
The requester join uses PostgREST syntax: requester:requester_company_id(legal_name)
